title: Licensing & data gate + per-dataset data manifest (open/synthetic only)
context: Every worked example in a stats-for-clinicians explainer must use only open-access, aggregate, de-identified, or synthetic data — individual patient data, dbGaP/EGA/individual-level biobanks, and controlled-access records are categorically excluded. Before any explainer can author a worked example, the project needs an objective, enforceable licensing and data gate: a written policy defining exactly which data classes are in bounds and which are out, a PASS criterion for dataset usability, and a committed per-dataset data manifest that records each dataset's source, version, license, de-identification status, and redistribution permissions. This gate prevents a scenario where a subtly non-open or individual-level dataset slips into an example.
objective: Author and commit the licensing & data gate policy and the per-dataset data manifest format, applying both to at least the pilot example datasets (hazard ratio and Kaplan-Meier examples), so that every subsequent explainer has an objective PASS/EXCLUDE criterion and a provenance trail for its example data.
acceptanceCriteria: The gate document enumerates the in-bounds data classes: synthetic/simulated data we generate; license-verified openly-licensed teaching survival datasets (de-identified by construction); open-access aggregate survival/incidence summaries (e.g. SEER/GLOBOCAN-style); pseudo-data synthetic-by-construction from CC-BY/CC0 published Kaplan-Meier figures only.

The gate document explicitly enumerates the hard exclusions with no exceptions: individual patient data; dbGaP/EGA/individual-level biobanks; controlled-access tiers; identifiable patient records including individual-level rows in article supplementary files.

The objective PASS criterion is stated: a dataset is usable only if its license is recorded (id + evidence/URL) and permits our teaching use and redistribution where applicable; missing or unparseable license = EXCLUDE, never default-allow.

A committed data manifest schema is defined and at least the pilot datasets (for hr-004 and km-006) are entered with: source, version/snapshot, license (id + evidence URL), de-identification status, and whether redistribution/derivatives are permitted.
Curve-derived pseudo-data policy is explicit: synthetic-by-construction from CC-BY/CC0 figures only; labelled as synthetic; never claimed as real patients; never re-identifying.
A policy statement forbids reproducing copyrighted figures verbatim; the required alternative is recreating equivalent figures from open/synthetic data.
The gate and manifest are committed to the project repo and referenced from the authoring template (template-002) as a required pre-flight check before any explainer's worked example can be drafted.
The licensing/provenance reviewer (from reviewer-001) has approved the gate document before it is merged.
output: A committed licensing & data gate policy document and a data manifest (schema + at least two pilot dataset entries) that together provide an objective PASS/EXCLUDE criterion for every example dataset used in the project. Referenced by all explainer authoring tasks.
